keyPoints: FCFS (First-Come-First-Served) - In FCFS, the processor is allocated to the process that has been waiting the least amount of time.
SJF (Shortest Job First) - In SJF, the processor is allocated to the process that has the shortest execution time.
Priority Scheduling - In Priority Scheduling, the processor is allocated to the process with the highest priority.
Round Robin Scheduling - In Round Robin Scheduling, the processor is allocated to the process that has been waiting the least amount of time, for a fixed time period.

Multilevel Feedback Queue (MLFQ) Scheduling - In MLFQ Scheduling, the processor is allocated to the process that has the shortest execution time, and the feedback is provided based on the performance of the process.
Time-Division Multiple-Access (TDMA) Scheduling - In TDMA Scheduling, the processor is allocated to the process that has the shortest execution time, and the processor is divided into time slots.
Earliest Deadline First (EDF) Scheduling - In EDF Scheduling, the processor is allocated to the process that has the earliest deadline.

Rate Monotonic Scheduling (RMS) Scheduling - In RMS Scheduling, the processor is allocated to the process that has the earliest deadline, and the priority is based on the rate at which the process executes.
